success

request.description:
## Register Investor

Registers a new investor account in the system. Upon successful registration, the server returns a `201 Created` response confirming the operation.

[https://bni.raiyansoft.net/#/signup?role=investor](https://bni.raiyansoft.net/#/signup?role=investor)

---

### HTTP Method & URL

```
POST {{baseUrl}}/v1/auth/register/investor

 ```

---

### Request Headers

| Key | Value |
| --- | --- |
| `Accept` | `application/json` |

---

### Request Body

**Content-Type:** `multipart/form-data`

| Field | Description | Example Value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `first_name` | Investor's first name | `ahmed` |
| `last_name` | Investor's last name | `nabeh` |
| `email` | Investor's email address | `a1@info.com` |
| `phone` | Investor's phone number | `58742145` |
| `password` | Account password | `123456789` |
| `investor_type` | Type of investor (e.g., angel, venture) | `angel` |
| `capital` | Total capital available to the investor | `100000` |
| `available_capital` | Capital currently available for investment | `10000` |
| `preferred_sector_id` | ID of the investor's preferred sector | `1` |
| `category_id` | ID of the investment category | `1` |
| `experience_level` | Investor's experience level (numeric scale) | `2` |
| `previous_investments_count` | Number of previous investments made | `5` |

> Additional fields may be required depending on the investor profile configuration. Refer to the saved examples for the full set of fields. 
  

---

### Response Format

Responses are returned as JSON. A successful registration returns HTTP `201 Created` with the following structure:

``` json
{
  "key": "success",
  "msg": "تم استرجاع البيانات بنجاح",
  "code": 201,
  "response_status": {
    "error": false,
    "validation_errors": []
  },
  "data": null
}

 ```

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `key` | Status key indicating the result (`success` or `error`) |
| `msg` | Human-readable message (may be in Arabic) |
| `code` | HTTP status code echoed in the response body |
| `response_status.error` | `false` on success, `true` on failure |
| `response_status.validation_errors` | Array of validation error messages, empty on success |
| `data` | Response payload; `null` for registration |

---

### Saved Examples

| Example Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **success** | Demonstrates a successful investor registration returning HTTP `201`. All required fields are provided and valid. |
| **validation error** | Demonstrates the response when one or more required fields are missing or invalid. The `response_status.error` field will be `true` and `validation_errors` will contain details about the failing fields. |

response.name: 422 - validation error
201 - created

request.description:
## Register Advertiser

Registers a new advertiser account in the system. This endpoint accepts the advertiser's personal and account details, validates the input, and creates a new account upon successful submission.

[https://bni.raiyansoft.net/#/signup?role=advertiser](https://bni.raiyansoft.net/#/signup?role=advertiser)

---

### Request Details

- **Method:** `POST`
    
- **URL:** `{{baseUrl}}/v1/auth/register/advertiser`
    
- **Content-Type:** `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`
    
- **Accept:** `application/json`
    

---

### Request Body Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `first_name` | string | Yes | The advertiser's first name. |
| `last_name` | string | Yes | The advertiser's last name. |
| `email` | string | Yes | A valid and unique email address for the advertiser account. |
| `password` | string | Yes | The account password. Must meet minimum security requirements. |
| `phone` | string | Yes | The advertiser's contact phone number. |
| `agreed_to_terms` | integer | Yes | Indicates acceptance of the terms and conditions. Pass `1` to confirm agreement. |

---

### Response Format

All responses are returned in JSON format.

**Success —** **`201 Created`**

Returned when the advertiser account is successfully created.

``` json
{
  "key": "...",
  "msg": "...",
  "code": 201,
  "response_status": "success",
  "data": { ... }
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `key` | string | A unique identifier or token associated with the response. |
| `msg` | string | A human-readable message describing the result. |
| `code` | integer | The HTTP status code of the response. |
| `response_status` | string | Indicates the overall status of the response (e.g., `"success"`). |
| `data` | object | Contains the newly created advertiser's account details. |

**Validation Error**

Returned when one or more required fields are missing or contain invalid values. The response will include details about which fields failed validation.

response.name: 422 - validation error
201 - created
validation error
validation error

request.description:
## List Investor Types

Retrieves a list of all available investor type enumerations. This endpoint is used to populate dropdowns or validate investor type values in other parts of the API.

---

### HTTP Method & URL

```
GET {{baseUrl}}/v1/general/investor-types
```

---

### Request Headers

| Header   | Value              | Description                        |
|----------|--------------------|------------------------------------|
| `Accept` | `application/json` | Specifies the expected response format |

---

### Response Structure

The response is a JSON object with the following top-level fields:

| Field             | Type    | Description                                                                 |
|-------------------|---------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `key`             | string  | A short identifier for the response status (e.g. `"success"`)              |
| `msg`             | string  | A human-readable message describing the result (may be in Arabic)          |
| `code`            | integer | HTTP-equivalent status code (e.g. `200`)                                   |
| `response_status` | object  | Contains error flag and validation errors (see below)                      |
| `data`            | array   | List of investor type enum entries (see below)                             |

#### `response_status` Object

| Field               | Type    | Description                                      |
|---------------------|---------|--------------------------------------------------|
| `error`             | boolean | `false` if the request was successful            |
| `validation_errors` | array   | List of validation error messages, if any        |

#### `data` Array Items

Each item in the `data` array represents an investor type enum entry:

| Field   | Type   | Description                                                              |
|---------|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `value` | string | The enum key used when submitting investor type in other API requests    |
| `name`  | string | The display name of the investor type                                    |
| `label` | string | The translation/localization key for the investor type label             |

---

### Example Response

```json
{
  "key": "success",
  "msg": "تم استرجاع البيانات بنجاح",
  "code": 200,
  "response_status": {
    "error": false,
    "validation_errors": []
  },
  "data": [
    {
      "value": "angel",
      "name": "Angel",
      "label": "enums.investor_type.Angel"
    },
    {
      "value": "company",
      "name": "Company",
      "label": "enums.investor_type.Company"
    },
    {
      "value": "crowdfunding",
      "name": "Crowdfunding",
      "label": "enums.investor_type.Crowdfunding"
    }
  ]
}
```

> **Note:** The `data` array contains all supported investor type enum entries. Use the `value` field when specifying an investor type in request payloads elsewhere in the API.

response.name: 200 - success

request.description:
## List Investor Experience Levels

Retrieves a list of all available investor experience levels supported by the system. This endpoint is used to populate dropdowns or selection fields where users are required to specify their investment experience.

---

### Request Details

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **Method** | `GET` |
| **URL** | `{{baseUrl}}/v1/general/investor-experience` |

**Headers**

| Key | Value |
|-----|-------|
| `Accept` | `application/json` |

**Request Body**

None — this endpoint does not require a request body.

---

### Response

**Status: `200 OK`**

```json
{
  "key": "success",
  "msg": "تم استرجاع البيانات بنجاح",
  "code": 200,
  "response_status": {
    "error": false,
    "validation_errors": []
  },
  "data": [
    {"value": "beginner", "name": "Beginner", "label": "مبتدئ"},
    {"value": "intermediate", "name": "Intermediate", "label": "متوسط"},
    {"value": "expert", "name": "Expert", "label": "خبير"}
  ]
}
```

#### Response Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `key` | `string` | A short status key indicating the result (e.g., `"success"`). |
| `msg` | `string` | A human-readable message describing the result (in Arabic). |
| `code` | `integer` | The HTTP status code mirrored in the response body. |
| `response_status` | `object` | Contains error flag and any validation errors. |
| `response_status.error` | `boolean` | `false` if the request was successful. |
| `response_status.validation_errors` | `array` | List of validation errors; empty on success. |
| `data` | `array` | List of investor experience level objects. |

#### `data` Array Item Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `value` | `string` | The machine-readable identifier used when submitting this level. |
| `name` | `string` | The English display name of the experience level. |
| `label` | `string` | The Arabic display label of the experience level. |

---

### Possible Experience Level Values

| `value` | `name` | `label` (Arabic) |
|---------|--------|------------------|
| `beginner` | Beginner | مبتدئ |
| `intermediate` | Intermediate | متوسط |
| `expert` | Expert | خبير |

response.name: 200 - success

request.description:
Retrieves a list of all **active preferred sectors**, returning each sector's `id` and localized `name`. This endpoint is typically used to populate sector selection dropdowns or filters within the application.

> **Note:** The `name` field is returned in the current app locale (translatable JSON: `ar`/`en`).

---

### Request

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **Method** | `GET` |
| **URL** | `{{baseUrl}}/v1/general/preferred-sectors` |
| **Accept** | `application/json` |

---

### Response Structure

On success, the API returns an HTTP `200 OK` with the following JSON structure:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `key` | `string` | A short status key (e.g., `"success"`). |
| `msg` | `string` | A human-readable status message (localized). |
| `code` | `integer` | HTTP-equivalent status code (e.g., `200`). |
| `response_status` | `object` | Contains error and validation metadata. |
| `response_status.error` | `boolean` | `false` when the request is successful. |
| `response_status.validation_errors` | `array` | Empty array on success; contains validation error details otherwise. |
| `data` | `array` | List of active preferred sector objects. |
| `data[].id` | `integer` | Unique identifier of the sector. |
| `data[].name` | `string` | Localized display name of the sector (`ar`/`en` based on app locale). |

---

### Sample 200 Response

```json
{
  "key": "success",
  "msg": "تم استرجاع البيانات بنجاح",
  "code": 200,
  "response_status": {
    "error": false,
    "validation_errors": []
  },
  "data": [
    { "id": 1, "name": "العقارات" },
    { "id": 2, "name": "التقنية" },
    { "id": 3, "name": "التجارة" },
    { "id": 4, "name": "الطاقة" }
  ]
}
```

> **HTTP 200** is returned on a successful retrieval of the sectors list.

## Who We Are

Retrieves the **"Who We Are"** page content, including the section's main title and description, as well as a list of highlight items (each with an image, title, and description).

[https://bni.raiyansoft.net/#/about](https://bni.raiyansoft.net/#/about)

---

### Request Headers

| Header | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Accept` | `application/json` | Specifies the expected response format. |
| `Accept-Language` | `{{lang}}` | The language code for the response content (e.g., `en`, `ar`). |

---

### Response

**Status:** `200 OK`

``` json
{
  "key": "success",
  "msg": "Data Retrieved Successfully",
  "code": 200,
  "response_status": {
    "error": false,
    "validation_errors": []
  },
  "data": {
    "whoWeAreSettings": {
      "title": "Who We Are",
      "description": "A brief description of the platform's mission and values."
    },
    "items": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "image": "<image_url>",
        "title": "Item Title",
        "description": "Item description text."
      }
    ]
  }
}

 ```

#### Response Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `key` | `string` | Status key, e.g. `success`. |
| `msg` | `string` | Human-readable status message. |
| `code` | `integer` | HTTP-equivalent status code. |
| `response_status.error` | `boolean` | `false` if the request was successful. |
| `response_status.validation_errors` | `array` | List of validation errors (empty on success). |
| `data.whoWeAreSettings.title` | `string` | The section's main title. |
| `data.whoWeAreSettings.description` | `string` | The section's main description text. |
| `data.items` | `array` | List of highlight items. |
| `data.items[].id` | `integer` | Unique identifier of the item. |
| `data.items[].image` | `string` | URL of the item's image. |
| `data.items[].title` | `string` | Title of the item. |
| `data.items[].description` | `string` | Description text of the item. |

---

### Variables Used

| Variable | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `{{baseUrl}}` | The base URL of the API (e.g., `https://api.example.com`). |
| `{{lang}}` | The language code for localized content (e.g., `en`, `ar`). |

response.name: who-we-are

request.description:
## Get Privacy Policy Retrieves the privacy policy content for the application. ### Request Headers | Header | Value | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | `Accept` | `application/json` | Specifies the response format | | `Accept-Language` | `{{lang}}` | The language code for the returned content (e.g. `en`, `ar`) | ### Response **200 OK** Returns the privacy policy content as an HTML string. ``` json { "key": "success", "msg": "Data Retrieved Successfully", "code": 200, "response_status": { "error": false, "validation_errors": [] }, "data": "

Privacy policy HTML content...

" } ``` ### Response Fields | Field | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | `key` | `string` | Status key, e.g. `success` | | `msg` | `string` | Human-readable status message | | `code` | `integer` | HTTP status code | | `response_status.error` | `boolean` | `false` if the request was successful | | `response_status.validation_errors` | `array` | List of validation errors, empty on success | | `data` | `string` | HTML string containing the privacy policy content | ### Variables | Variable | Description | | --- | --- | | `{{baseUrl}}` | The base URL of the API server | | `{{lang}}` | The language code used to localize the response content |

response.name: privacy-policy

request.description:
## Terms and Conditions Retrieves the terms and conditions content for the application. ### Method `GET` ### Endpoint `{{baseUrl}}/v1/general/terms-and-conditions` ### Headers | Header | Value | Description | |---|---|---| | `Accept` | `application/json` | Specifies that the response should be in JSON format. | | `Accept-Language` | `{{lang}}` | The language code for the desired response language (e.g., `en`, `ar`). Controlled by the `lang` environment variable. | ### Response **Success (200 OK)** Returns a JSON object containing the terms and conditions content as an HTML string. ```json { "key": "success", "msg": "Data retrieved successfully", "code": 200, "response_status": { "error": false, "validation_errors": [] }, "data": "

Terms and conditions content...

" } ``` | Field | Type | Description | |---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Status key indicating the result of the operation. | | `msg` | `string` | A human-readable message describing the result. | | `code` | `integer` | HTTP status code. | | `response_status.error` | `boolean` | `false` if the request was successful. | | `response_status.validation_errors` | `array` | List of validation errors, empty on success. | | `data` | `string` | The terms and conditions content as an HTML string. | ### Notes - Use the `lang` environment variable to control the language of the returned content. - The `data` field contains raw HTML that can be rendered directly in a web view.

response.name: terms-and-conditions

request.description:
## List Investors (Public Directory)

Returns a **paginated list of active investors** from the public directory. Results can be filtered by investor type, available capital range, investment experience, and preferred sector.

[https://bni.raiyansoft.net/#/investors](https://bni.raiyansoft.net/#/investors)

> **Public endpoint** — No authentication is required to access this endpoint. 
  

---

### Headers

| Header | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Accept` | `application/json` | Must be set to receive a JSON response. |
| `Accept-Language` | `ar` | `en` | Controls the language of localized labels (e.g. investor type labels, sector names, experience levels). Defaults to Arabic if omitted. |

---

### Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `investor_type` | string | No | Filter by investor type. Valid values are returned by `GET /v1/general/investor-types`. |
| `min_capital` | integer | No | Minimum available capital in **Kuwaiti Dinar (KWD)**, inclusive. |
| `max_capital` | integer | No | Maximum available capital in **Kuwaiti Dinar (KWD)**, inclusive. Must be `>= min_capital` when both are provided. |
| `investor_experience` | string | No | Filter by investment experience level. Valid values are returned by `GET /v1/general/investor-experience`. |
| `preferred_sector_id` | integer | No | Filter by preferred sector ID. Valid IDs are returned by `GET /v1/general/preferred-sectors`. |
| `page` | integer | No | Page number for pagination. Defaults to `1`. |
| `per_page` | integer | No | Number of results per page. Defaults to `10`, maximum is `100`. |

---

### Response Structure

**Success —** **`200 OK`**

``` json
{
  "key": "success",
  "msg": "...",
  "code": 200,
  "response_status": {
    "error": false,
    "validation_errors": []
  },
  "data": {
    "investors": [
      {
        "id": 4,
        "display_id": "USR-4",
        "investor_type": { "value": "angel", "label": "..." },
        "available_capital": 300000,
        "focus_sector": { "id": 3, "name": "..." },
        "investment_experience": { "value": "intermediate", "label": "..." },
        "image": "https://..."
      }
    ],
    "pagination": {
      "current_page": 1,
      "last_page": 1,
      "per_page": 10,
      "total": 2
    }
  }
}

 ```

#### Investor Object Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | integer | Internal investor ID. |
| `display_id` | string | Human-readable investor identifier (e.g. `USR-4`). |
| `investor_type` | object | Investor classification. `value` is the raw enum; `label` is localized. |
| `available_capital` | integer | Available investment capital in **KWD**. |
| `focus_sector` | object | Preferred investment sector (`id` + localized `name`). |
| `investment_experience` | object | Experience level. `value` is the raw enum; `label` is localized. |
| `image` | string | URL of the investor's profile image. |

#### Pagination Object Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `current_page` | integer | The current page number. |
| `last_page` | integer | Total number of pages. |
| `per_page` | integer | Items returned per page. |
| `total` | integer | Total number of matching investors. |

---

### Notes

- Capital values (`min_capital`, `max_capital`, `available_capital`) are always in **Kuwaiti Dinar (KWD)**.
    
- Use the `Accept-Language` header with `ar` or `en` to receive localized enum labels and sector names in the desired language.
    
- Use the reference endpoints (`/v1/general/investor-types`, `/v1/general/investor-experience`, `/v1/general/preferred-sectors`) to retrieve valid filter values before querying this endpoint.
    
- The `{{lang}}` variable in the `Accept-Language` header is resolved from the active Postman environment.

response.name: List investors (public directory)

request.description:
## Packages ### Overview Retrieves the available subscription packages for the platform. This endpoint is used to display a packages/pricing page to the user, including page-level content (title and description) and a list of available plans with their details. The API is designed for an investment/real-estate platform and returns content in the language specified by the `Accept-Language` header. [https://bni.raiyansoft.net/#/pricing](https://bni.raiyansoft.net/#/pricing) --- ### Request Details **Method:** `GET` **URL:** `{{baseUrl}}/v1/general/packages` #### Headers | Key | Value | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | `Accept` | `application/json` | Specifies that the client expects a JSON response. | | `Accept-Language` | `{{lang}}` | Sets the language for the response content (e.g., `ar` for Arabic, `en` for English). | #### Variables | Variable | Description | | --- | --- | | `{{baseUrl}}` | The base URL of the API server (e.g., `https://api.example.com`). Defined in the active environment. | | `{{lang}}` | The desired response language code. Defined in the active environment. | --- ### Response Structure The response follows a standard envelope format used across the API. | Field | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | `key` | `string` | A machine-readable status key (e.g., `"success"`). | | `msg` | `string` | A human-readable status message, localized based on `Accept-Language`. | | `code` | `number` | The HTTP-equivalent status code (e.g., `200`). | | `response_status.error` | `boolean` | `false` if the request was successful, `true` otherwise. | | `response_status.validation_errors` | `array` | A list of validation error messages, if any. Empty on success. | | `data.pageSettings.title` | `string` | The localized title for the packages page (e.g., \*"Choose your access level"\*). | | `data.pageSettings.description` | `string` | A localized subtitle or description shown on the packages page. | | `data.packages` | `array` | A list of available subscription package objects. | | `data.packages[].id` | `number` | Unique identifier for the package. | | `data.packages[].name` | `string` | Localized display name of the package. | | `data.packages[].description` | `string` | HTML-formatted list of features included in the package. | | `data.packages[].price_monthly` | `string` | Localized monthly price string (e.g., `"19 د.ك / شهر"`). | | `data.packages[].can_register` | `boolean` | Whether the current user is eligible to subscribe to this package. | | `data.packages[].is_subscribed` | `boolean` | Whether the current user is already subscribed to this package. | --- ### Sample Response ``` json { "key": "success", "msg": "تم استرجاع البيانات بنجاح", "code": 200, "response_status": { "error": false, "validation_errors": [] }, "data": { "pageSettings": { "title": "اختر مستوى الوصول", "description": "افتح فرص الاستثمار الموثقة بخطة تناسب أهداف محفظتك." }, "packages": [ { "id": 1, "name": "أساسي", "description": "
  • تصفح المشاريع
  • دعم أساسي
  • ٥ مفضلة
", "price_monthly": "19 د.ك / شهر", "can_register": false, "is_subscribed": false } ] } } ```

response.name: packages

request.description:
## Send Investor Interest Request

Sends an interest request from a company to a specific investor. This endpoint allows a company to express formal interest in an investor, initiating a connection or engagement workflow between the two parties.

---

### Authentication & Authorization

This endpoint is scoped under the `/v1/company/` path, meaning it requires a **company-level authentication token**. Ensure the `company_token` is included in the request (typically via an `Authorization` header or as configured in the active environment). Requests made without a valid company token will be rejected.

---

### Request Details

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| **Method** | `POST` |
| **Endpoint** | `{{baseUrl}}/v1/company/investor-interest-requests` |

#### Headers

| Header | Value | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Accept` | `application/json` | Specifies that the response should be in JSON format. |
| `Accept-Language` | `{{lang}}` | Sets the preferred language for the response. Resolved from the `lang` environment variable. |

#### Body Parameters (form-data)

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `investor_id` | `text` | ✅ Yes | The unique identifier of the investor to whom the interest request is being sent. |

---

### Use Case

Use this endpoint when a company wants to signal interest in a particular investor. This could trigger a notification to the investor, create a pending connection record, or initiate a review process — depending on the platform's business logic. The `investor_id` must correspond to a valid, existing investor in the system.
